title: mRemoteNG crashes if panel containing connection(s) is closed
author: C4rb0n
body_snippet:
<!--- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
When connections belonging to more than one panel are open (thus making the panel tabs appear), if a panel is closed, by clicking the "X" or right-clicking and choosing "X Close" on the panel tab itself, mRemoteNG crashes with the following exception:

   at System.Windows.Forms.Control.MarshaledInvoke(Control caller, Delegate method, Object[] args, Boolean synchronous)
   at System.Windows.Forms.Control.Invoke(Delegate meth...

iterations.description: Application crashes when a panel containing connections is closed, indicating a critical bug in UI panel lifecycle management or resource disposal.

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: This could be related to #2118, just caused manually and not when closing the Window itself...

comments.author: PetbkA
comments.snippet:
I guess the problem is with connections that require Putty (Telnet, SSH etc.). Tried with RDP, VNC, Telnet and SSH connections and only the latter two are causing crash. Exception is the same:

```
Exception Message
"Cannot access a disposed object.
Object name: 'ConnectionWindow'."

Stack trace
   at System.Windows.Forms.Control.MarshaledInvoke(Control caller, Delegate method, Object[] args, Boolean synchronous)
   at System.Windows.Forms.Control.Invoke(Delegate method, Object[] args)...

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
Likely addressed by close-panel stability package in PR #3106 (same panel-close/disposed connection path). 
 
Please retest on latest fork RC candidate build; if no longer reproducible, this can be closed as duplicate of #3069.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
This appears to match the same ConnectionWindow.Prot_Event_Closed disposed-object close-path as #3069. Candidate upstream fix package is PR #3106: PR #3106 on `mRemoteNG/mRemoteNG` After merge in parent, please retest on a build that includes that commit; if still reproducible with exact steps/logs, keep this issue open, otherwise close as duplicate of #3069.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

notes:
AI triage (gemini): Application crashes when a panel containing connections is closed, indicating a critical bug in UI panel lifecycle management or resource disposal.
Approach: Investigate UI panel closing logic in `frmMain` and connection tab disposal to ensure proper resource release and null reference handling.
